prose:
Method provenance — fukui-reactivity descriptors for acrolein
1. Property: Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor; identification of the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic atomic sites.
2. Level of theory: DFT tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted RIJK) in PySCF 2.x.
3. Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry supplied as `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz`; no re-optimization (single-point energies at N, N+1 and N–1 electron counts).
4. Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
5. Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet for the N-electron reference (charge 0, multiplicity 1); corresponding doublets for the ±1 states (charge +1, mult 2; charge –1, mult 2).
6. Software/backend: chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill (PySCF engine, B3LYP functional, def2-TZVP basis, density fitting on by default).
7. Convergence & warnings: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity status "ok" (trustworthy = true).  Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
   • sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
   • sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
   • common header present
   No SCF failures reported.

Live run log: the engine streamed its output to the standard chemkit live log file in the caller’s working directory (named `fukui_<timestamp>.out`); this file was written during execution and can be inspected with `tail -f` for full SCF and Mulliken-charge details.  The structured result JSON is at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmphlx7vezp/live.json`; plot image saved as
`/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/argo_o3/20260703-185616_acrolein_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.

Headline results (Mulliken scheme):
• Most electrophilic atom: C4 (index 3, symbol C) with f⁺ = 0.64232102.
• Most nucleophilic atom: O1 (index 0, symbol O) with f⁻ = 0.39388616.

Full Fukui vectors (per atom, O–C–C–C–H–H–H–H):
  f⁺ = [0.40136, 0.02226, 0.43405, 0.64232, –0.15726, –0.20720, –0.06212, –0.07341]
  f⁻ = [0.39389, 0.00599, 0.09140, 0.08737, 0.08596, 0.18568, 0.08449, 0.06523]
  f⁰ = [0.39763, 0.01412, 0.26272, 0.36485, –0.03565, –0.01076, 0.01118, –0.00409]
  Dual descriptor Δf = [0.00748, 0.01627, 0.34264, 0.55495, –0.24322, –0.39288, –0.14660, –0.13863]

The dual descriptor confirms electrophilic reactivity is localized predominantly at the β-carbon (C4), while nucleophilic attack is favored at the carbonyl oxygen, in line with chemical intuition for α,β-unsaturated carbonyls.
